headline: Maya, your gut looks like a busy, fiber-loving community with several well-known beneficial bacteria thriving — overall a reassuring picture with a couple of gentle things worth noticing.

findings.claim: You have a strong population of one of the gut's standout 'good' bacteria, sitting comfortably in the healthy range.
findings.science_underneath: Faecalibacterium prausnitzii is a leading butyrate producer, which fuels the cells lining your colon and acts as an anti-inflammatory signal. Healthy adults typically run around 3–15%, so your level is right in that band.

findings.claim: Your most abundant bug is a fiber-cracking keystone species — a good sign for a plant-forward diet like yours.
findings.science_underneath: Ruminococcus bromii is the primary breaker-down of resistant starch (from things like cooled rice and potatoes, legumes, and whole grains). By cracking that starch open, it lets the wider community ferment it into beneficial short-chain fatty acids.

findings.claim: You carry a healthy share of a classic probiotic-type bacterium that helps support your gut barrier.
findings.science_underneath: Bifidobacterium longum ferments dietary fibers into lactate and acetate and helps crowd out less friendly microbes. The Bifidobacterium genus is often around 1–10% in healthy adults, and yours is on the generous side.

findings.claim: A lactate-recycling specialist is more prominent than it usually is — a gentle thing to keep an eye on rather than a worry.
findings.science_underneath: Veillonella doesn't eat sugar; it tidies up lactate made by other microbes and turns it into propionate and acetate. It's usually a minor member (under ~1–2%), so a higher level like yours simply reflects a lactate-rich community and is worth a soft look over time.

findings.claim: Another short-chain-fatty-acid producer is contributing to your gut-lining nutrition.
findings.science_underneath: Megasphaera massiliensis makes short-chain fatty acids including butyrate and valerate from a range of substrates, which is generally viewed as supportive of the colon lining.

findings.claim: Your flexible 'generalist' backbone bacteria are present at a modest, normal level.
findings.science_underneath: The Bacteroides group (including Bacteroides stercoris, B. fragilis, B. thetaiotaomicron, Phocaeicola dorei, and Parabacteroides distasonis) are versatile eaters that break down both fibers and proteins. They're a normal backbone of the adult gut, often 10–40% collectively.

findings.claim: You have a little of a mucus-associated bacterium, but only at a low background level — nothing remarkable here.
findings.science_underneath: Mediterraneibacter gnavus can become inflammatory when it blooms to high levels, but at the low background level you have (under ~2%) it's an unremarkable, common resident.

findings.claim: A bacterium often seen as a 'recovery marker' is present, which tends to track a settled, balanced community.
findings.science_underneath: Sellimonas intestinalis has been proposed as a marker of gut homeostasis — its presence often tracks the community sitting in balance, though this is an emerging idea rather than proof.

findings.claim: Your overall diversity score is on the moderate-to-lower side — and that is not a problem by itself.
findings.science_underneath: Diversity summarizes how many species you have and how evenly they're spread. Low diversity is not automatically unhealthy — some healthy people on plant-forward or restricted diets run lower diversity while having excellent gut function, so it's just one lens among many.

findings.claim: Your community is geared toward making the short-chain fatty acids that come from fermenting fiber.
findings.science_underneath: Short-chain fatty acids like butyrate, propionate, and acetate are made when gut bacteria ferment dietary fiber; butyrate in particular fuels your colon lining and has anti-inflammatory effects. Your mix of keystone fiber-degraders and butyrate producers supports this whole chain.

footer: Maya is a synthetic demo patient. This report is for informational and demonstration purposes only and is not medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Knowledge-pack citations are marked for verification in a human review gate.
